title: Prumnopitys ladei
taxonomy: Prumnopitys ladei is classified as a species of coniferous tree in the family Podocarpaceae, native to New Zealand. It is a member of the genus Prumnopitys, which consists of several species of conifers. Prumnopitys ladei is often referred to as the New Zealand yellow pine or tōtara, although it is not a true pine. This species is closely related to other conifers in the family Podocarpaceae, such as the southern raoupe or Prumnopitys ferruginea. Prumnopitys ladei is a significant component of New Zealand's native forests.
uses: Prumnopitys ladei, also known as the matai, is a plant native to New Zealand with a rich history of traditional use.  In traditional Maori medicine, the leaves and bark of the matai are used for their medicinal properties.  The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues.  The leaves are also used to make a tea that is believed to help with digestive problems and sore throats.  Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Prumnopitys ladei.

intro: Prumnopitys ladei, also known as the New Zealand native kauri tree's congener, is a species of coniferous tree belonging to the family Podocarpaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to provide relief from conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues.
